body_txt: Generally speaking, there's no real morality attached to the common Sweet Tooth . It's just that their preferences just happen to lean towards the more saccharine end of the taste spectrum, so the trope is largely neutral... unless you're one of these guys. Here, a taste for confection, chocolate, or other sweet foods is clearly identified as a villainous trait. The reasons may vary: perhaps it's being used to highlight the villain's hedonistic side ; it can be used as a Trademark Favorite Food in order to identify the villain while disguised; if Real Men Hate Sugar in this setting, it may even be used to set them apart from other, more heroic characters. A possible trait of the Villainous Glutton and the Psychopathic Manchild . In historical settings this may overlap with Aristocrats Are Evil , as back in the day, white sugar was so expensive that the only people who could eat sugary sweets on a day-to-day basis were nobility or royalty. Furthermore, as cane sugar was heavily associated with the historic Atlantic slave trade and conditions on the sugar plantations were horrific even by the standards of the time, there could also be some overlap with Slavery Is a Special Kind of Evil See also Genius Sweet Tooth , in which the sugary preferences are used to identify intelligence rather than villainy. Contrast Straight Edge Evil , in which a villainous character abstains from unhealthy habits, which often includes sweets; and Mascots Love Sugar (critters love sugar, which adds to their overall cuteness). Not related to Hitler Ate Sugar .
